prompt: Transform the attached image into a drawing that looks like it was made by a very young child, around 3 to 4 years old, using crayons. The drawing should feel silly, clumsy, and adorably bad, as if the child was trying to draw what they saw but only vaguely understood the subject. The lines should not be straight or clean. Avoid adult-level composition, realistic proportions, accurate anatomy, detailed shading, or polished illustration. Make it look awkward, naive, uneven, and a little random, like it was drawn from a toddler’s point of view. It is okay if the result looks messy, strange, funny, and not fully accurate to the original image. Style: childish crayon drawing, rough scribbly lines, uneven shapes, simple colors, imperfect proportions, loose interpretation of the attached image.
